title: passiflora antioquiensis var. antioquiensis
intro: Passiflora antioquiensis var. antioquiensis, also known as the Yellow Granadilla or Yellow Passion Flower, is a type of flowering plant native to the Andean region of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and sedative effects, and is often used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and menstrual cramps. The Yellow Granadilla is rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, providing various health benefits such as reducing stress and promoting relaxation. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal effects.
